TypeSignature.Value: .class public interface auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit IOrderedEnumerable`1<+ TElement> implements class System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1<!TElement>, class System.Collections.IEnumerable
Public Interface IOrderedEnumerable(Of Out TElement) Implements IEnumerable(Of Out TElement)
type IOrderedEnumerable<'Element> = interface interface seq<'Element> interface IEnumerable
generic <typename TElement> public interface class IOrderedEnumerable : System::Collections::Generic::IEnumerable<TElement>

Docs.typeparam: The type of the elements of the sequence.

remarks.format:
## Remarks This type is enumerable because it inherits from <xref:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1>.
The extension methods <xref:System.Linq.Enumerable.ThenBy*> and <xref:System.Linq.Enumerable.ThenByDescending*> operate on objects of type <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable`1>. An object of type <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable`1> can be obtained by calling one of the primary sort methods, <xref:System.Linq.Enumerable.OrderBy*> or <xref:System.Linq.Enumerable.OrderByDescending*>, which return an <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable`1>. <xref:System.Linq.Enumerable.ThenBy*> and <xref:System.Linq.Enumerable.ThenByDescending*>, the subordinate sort methods, in turn also return an object of type <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable`1>. This design allows for any number of consecutive calls to <xref:System.Linq.Enumerable.ThenBy*> or <xref:System.Linq.Enumerable.ThenByDescending*>, where each call performs a subordinate ordering on the sorted data returned from the previous call.
## Examples The following example demonstrates how to perform a primary and secondary ordering on an array of strings. It also demonstrates that the resulting <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable`1> is enumerable.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Linq/IOrderedEnumerableTElement/Overview/IOrderedEnumerable.cs" interactive="try-dotnet-method" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Linq/IOrderedEnumerableTElement/Overview/IOrderedEnumerable.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: public System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable<out TElement> CreateOrderedEnumerable<TKey> (Func<out TElement,TKey> keySelector, System.Collections.Generic.IComparer<TKey>? comparer, bool descending);

.method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance class System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable`1<!TElement> CreateOrderedEnumerable<TKey>(class System.Func`2<!TElement, !!TKey> keySelector, class System.Collections.Generic.IComparer`1<!!TKey> comparer, bool descending) cil managed
Public Function CreateOrderedEnumerable(Of TKey) (keySelector As Func(Of Out TElement, TKey), comparer As IComparer(Of TKey), descending As Boolean) As IOrderedEnumerable(Of Out TElement)
abstract member CreateOrderedEnumerable : Func<'Element, 'Key> * System.Collections.Generic.IComparer<'Key> * bool -> System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable<'Element>

Docs.typeparam: The type of the key produced by .
Docs.param: The used to extract the key for each element.
The used to compare keys for placement in the returned sequence.
to sort the elements in descending order; to sort the elements in ascending order.
Docs.summary: Performs a subsequent ordering on the elements of an according to a key.
Docs.returns: An whose elements are sorted according to a key.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The functionality provided by this method is like that provided by <xref:System.Linq.Enumerable.ThenBy*> or <xref:System.Linq.Enumerable.ThenByDescending*>, depending on whether `descending` is `true` or `false`. They both perform a subordinate ordering of an already sorted sequence of type <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable`1>.
## Examples The following code example demonstrates how to use <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable`1.CreateOrderedEnumerable*> to perform a secondary ordering on an <xref:System.Linq.IOrderedEnumerable`1>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Linq/IOrderedEnumerableTElement/Overview/IOrderedEnumerable.cs" interactive="try-dotnet-method" id="Snippet2"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Linq/IOrderedEnumerableTElement/Overview/IOrderedEnumerable.vb" id="Snippet2":::
